calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-20-2026 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-05-27 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Mar-06-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Appearances:
Mari Bandoma Callado of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.3950 / mari.bandoma@sfcityatty.org) for defendant and moving party;
Taleah Phullips and Chandler C. Roth of Gomez Trial Attorneys (619.237.3490 / talea@getgomez.com / chandler@getgomez.com) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary adjudication of the first, third, fourth, sixth, eighth and ninth causes of action is denied as to all six causes of action.

Plaintiff Jonathon Salerno has presented sufficient evidence to create a triable dispute as to each of the six causes of action.

As to the first cause of action for disability discrimination, the third cause of action for FEHA retaliation, and the sixth cause of action for failure to prevent discrimination, harassment or retaliation, there are triable issues whether the reassignment of Salerno from acting senior stationary engineer to stationary engineer is an adverse action and was motivated by Salerno's disability and his request to accommodate that disability.

As to the fourth cause of action for disability harassment, there are triable issues whether the conduct of co-worker Ruedell Walker was motivated by Salerno's disability and created a hostile work environment.

As to the eighth cause of action for failure to accommodate and the ninth cause of action for failure to engage in an interactive process, there are triable issues whether, in revoking Salerno's permission to have an emotional support animal and in reassigning him from acting senior stationary engineer to stationary engineer, the City failed to reasonably accommodate Salerno's disability and failed to act in good faith in an interactive process regarding Salerno's disability.

In making the above rulings, the court relied only on admissible evidence. Salerno is required to submit a proposed order consistent with the requirements of CCP 437c.

Further, the court overrules defendant's objection number 10.

Counsel stipulate to the proposed order presented in court, which is signed.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Deborah Fuqua, CSR 12948 (T: 415.810.7170, E: dfuquacsr@gmail.com) (301/HEK)
